title: P2.3: Verify references/text/*:line locators in canonical content
description:
Phase 2 step P2.3 per MIGRATION_PLAN.md:153. For each  line-locator citation in canonical content (summary.tex, GLOSSARY.md, CONVENTIONS.md, ERRATA.md, CITATION_INDEX.md), verify that the cited line exists and the cited text matches. Any mismatch is a stop condition.

Plan-vs-reality note (per HANDOFF gotcha #13): the plan says 'For each SRC-* line locator in references/manifest/SOURCES.md' — but the manifest has only file paths + hashes, NOT per-line locators. Line-locators live in canonical content citations (when they exist) and informally in cad-aux.md §5. Current canonical content has zero such citations (P3 will add them as part of \unchecked discharge). P2.3 builds the verification infrastructure as a pre-registered gate; expected first non-vacuous PASS at P3.2+.

Validation gates: M (script runs), C (2-source: cited line + manifest), I.
close_reason: P2.3 complete: line-locator verification gate built (scripts/check-references-line-locators.py). Vacuous PASS (0 locators in canonical content; gate fires in earnest at Phase 3). Plan-vs-reality note: the plan envisaged locators in references/manifest/SOURCES.md but the manifest has only hashes; locators (when they exist) live in canonical content citations.

title: P2.2: SHA256 verification of references/ against manifest
description: Phase 2 step P2.2 per MIGRATION_PLAN.md:152. Recompute SHA256 for every file under references/; compare to hashes in references/manifest/SOURCES.md. Any mismatch is a stop condition. Validation gates: M (script passes), C (2-source: file hash + manifest record), I (idempotent — re-running gives same result).
close_reason: P2.2 complete: 33/33 SHA256 hashes verified (16 primary + 17 extracted text). Zero mismatches. Verification script committed to scripts/check-references-sha256.py for future reproducibility (matches scripts/check-canonical-consistency.sh pattern in CLAUDE.md).

close_reason: P2.1 complete: 38 source files copied (14 PDFs + 1 manifest + 16 .txt/.md + 7 deprecated/.txt). cp -a preserved permissions/timestamps; diff -rq confirms zero divergence. .gitkeep removed (no longer needed; directory has 28M of real content). Sizes match (28M source, 28M dest).

title: P2: Phase 2 — Provenance infrastructure (validator imports)
description:
Phase 2 per stocktake/MIGRATION_PLAN.md:143-160. Import external validators (references PDFs, literature DB, citation index) before any mathematical content.

User approved Phase 2 unblock on 2026-05-16 after reading the P1.9 audit (verdict APPROVE FOR PHASE 2, zero CRITICAL/MAJOR).

8 sub-steps:
- P2.1: Copy cft-anyons-deprecated/references/ -> cft-anyons/references/.
- P2.2: Recompute SHA256 for every file, compare to manifest/SOURCES.md.
- P2.3: Verify every SRC-* line locator contains the cited text.
- P2.4: Copy microscopic-mobile-anyons/literature/ -> cft-anyons/literature/.
- P2.5: Verify literature/db/papers.sqlite integrity (PRAGMA + row counts vs SYNTHESIS.md).
- P2.6: Copy literature CLI to scripts/lit/. Confirm 'lit status' works.
- P2.7: Append literature DB / CLI mention to CONVENTIONS; update PROVENANCE hashes.
- P2.8: Build scripts/build-citation-index.py and emit CITATION_INDEX.md.

Most steps are mechanical (Rule 4 exempt). Per CLAUDE.md: file moves, hash checks, sqlite integrity checks, applying user-verbatim-specified copies are all mechanical.
close_reason: all steps complete

description:

Changes:

close_reason: P1.11 complete: PRD.md refreshed v0 -> v1. Update PROVENANCE.md from stub to populated state with a Phase 1 canonical baseline: which content is now canonical (GLOSSARY.md, CONVENTIONS.md, ERRATA.md, summary.tex, supporting stocktake reports), with source paths + SHA256 hashes + provenance chains.

For each Phase 1 canonical artifact, record:
- Source path (in-repo or external project)
- SHA256 at this commit
- Migration step that authored / last touched it

Per P1.9 audit follow-up #6: include the P1.9 audit verdict in the GLOSSARY/CONVENTIONS entries so future audits can read the prior verdict.

close_reason: P1.10 complete: PROVENANCE.md refreshed from stub to populated state. Phase 1 canonical baseline section (summary table + per-artifact entries) records every canonical artifact's SHA256, last-touch commit, P-step, source, and provenance chain. Validation gates per plan: M (entries follow schema; all hashes recomputable via e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  -), I (hash-stable post-landing). No R gate per the plan (P1.10 is mechanical/integrity only; no review subagent). 288 lines.

title: P1.9: Definitional gate audit (Opus reads GLOSSARY+CONVENTIONS end-to-end)
description:
Phase 1 step P1.9 per stocktake/MIGRATION_PLAN.md:133. Spawn an Opus 4.7 hostile audit subagent that reads GLOSSARY.md (1790 lines, 49 entries: 48 in §A from summary.tex + 1 in §B mobile-Fock) and CONVENTIONS.md (552 lines, 10 entries a-j) end-to-end and produces a report flagging:
- Any term used in GLOSSARY without its own entry.
- Any internal inconsistency between entries.
- Any conflict with summary.tex.
- Any cross-link [[slug]] that doesn't resolve.
- Any forward-pointer [P1.6(x)] from GLOSSARY that doesn't resolve to a CONVENTIONS entry.
- Any silent disagreement with stocktake/reports/opus-hilbert-bridge.md (the authoritative Hilbert-space framing source).

Output to stocktake/reports/opus-glossary-audit.md.

Validation gates: M (audit report produced); R (user must read and either approve or request edits before Phase 2 begins).

**Critical:** this is the gate before Phase 2 starts (provenance/validator imports). Per MIGRATION_PLAN line 137-139: 'Stop and confirm with user before proceeding to Phase 2.' The audit's verdict is binding on whether Phase 2 unblocks.
close_reason: P1.9 complete: Opus 4.7 hostile-audit subagent read GLOSSARY + CONVENTIONS end-to-end. Verdict APPROVE FOR PHASE 2 (zero CRITICAL, zero MAJOR, 8 MINOR/NIT). All 5 MINOR cleanups applied in same commit (GLOSSARY Status refresh; CONVENTIONS Status refresh; 7 future-tense forward-pointers → past tense / [P1.X(letter)]; archive path clarified; def:Q sweep wording fixed). 3 NITs (findings 6-8: orphan entries, schema TBD-by-design, asymmetric cross-links) deferred as optional polish. Phase 2 unblocked pending user read-and-approve of the audit. LB-4 (cft-anyons-?) filed for post-snapshot-import re-validation. Report at stocktake/reports/opus-glossary-audit.md.

Other MMA bullets get either a substantive mapping or 'Not implemented in MMA' note with brief justification.

Pattern from P1.7 (commit d8c0a40): Python-script-then-Write for bulk edits, then verify SA canonical bodies are still byte-verbatim via D-gate replay; spawn Opus reviewer subagent; apply minor edits; commit with full provenance footer.
close_reason: P1.8 complete: 45 MMA bullets populated + conv:basics Aliases updated. Hostile Opus reviewer verdict APPROVE WITH MINOR EDITS, all 5 edits applied. title: P1.2: ERRATA entry for unanimous lem:binary-Z amplitudes-vs-probabilities bug + verify/apply summary.tex fix
description: Per MIGRATION_PLAN Phase 1, step P1.2. The lem:binary-Z bug (chat-4 source displays probabilities Z(x)Z(y)/Z(L) but isometric morphism requires amplitudes sqrt{Z(x)Z(y)/Z(L)}) was unanimously flagged by all 4 hostile reviews (review_1_algebra Issue1, review_2_categorical Issue11, review_3_cft Issue17, review_4_consistency). Tasks: (1) confirm what the actual unanimous flag was by reading all 4 reviews; (2) confirm whether the fix is already applied in summary.tex or still pending — the in-doc warning at line 1583 acknowledges the bug and claims 'the square-rooted formulas above are the corrected version'; (3) if pending, apply the fix; (4) add ERRATA.md entry pointing to all 4 reviewer issues with full provenance. Validation: M (pdflatex still builds, same TOC), D (fix changes ONLY displayed coefficients, no surrounding text), C (4 reviewers agree), R (in-doc warning already authored — but external hostile review still required per CLAUDE.md Rule 4 for any summary.tex edit). Parent: cft-anyons-k3s.
close_reason: P1.2 complete: ERRATA entry added for lem:binary-Z amplitudes-vs-probabilities bug. Fix was already applied to summary.tex before this repo was initialised (no summary.tex changes in this commit); ERRATA formalises the audit trail. 3 of 4 hostile reviews flagged the bug (review_1 Issue 1 + review_2 Issue 11 + review_3 Issue 17); review_4 silent on this lemma. Opus reviewer APPROVE WITH MINOR EDITS — both applied. Report at stocktake/reports/opus-errata-p1.2-review.md.

title: build-citation-index.py: line lists are author-name occurrences, not \unchecked positions
description: P3.2.a implementer (commit 3403ca5) discovered: CITATION_INDEX's per-atom 'summary.tex lines' field lists lines where the author SURNAME appears (via PATTERNS list grep in build-citation-index.py:336-368), NOT lines where \unchecked macro is actually located. For osborne-stottmeister: CITATION_INDEX listed 4 lines (2303/2381/2446/2466) but the actual \unchecked macros are at 2304/2380/2468 (3 sites). Line 2446 has no \unchecked at all — it's an item in a \section*{Reference status} enumerate covered by the blanket line-2437 declaration. Impact: (1) misleading schema; (2) P3.2.b/c briefings must compensate; (3) P3.4 count-delta verification needs to compare actual \unchecked-macro counts only. Recommend: distinguish 'author-name-occurrence lines' from '\unchecked-attribution lines' in the schema and emission. Bundled with cft-anyons-6ku (YAML promotion) for Phase 3 cleanup pass.

title: CITATION_INDEX/build-citation-index.py: SRC-STRING-NET maps to wrong Levin-Wen paper
description: P3.0 implementer (commit 6d9ca8c) found: SRC-STRING-NET PDF is cond-mat/0510613 (Levin-Wen 2007, 'Detecting topological order in a ground state wave function'). But CITATION_INDEX.md cross-references it to lit DB id=324 (cond-mat/0404617, Levin-Wen 2005 — different paper, same authors/topic). Need to verify which Levin-Wen paper is in lit DB, and either fix the hardcoded mapping in build-citation-index.py or acquire the other paper. Discovered via Rule 7 ground-truth check of references/text/LevinWenDetectingTopologicalOrder.txt.

title: CITATION_INDEX/build-citation-index.py: SRC-OSBORNE-CONTINUUM maps to wrong lit DB paper ID
description: P3.0 implementer (commit 6d9ca8c) found: SRC-OSBORNE-CONTINUUM PDF is arXiv:1901.06124 (Osborne single-author 2019, lit DB id=519). But CITATION_INDEX.md:154 maps it to paper#521 (arXiv:2107.13834, which is SRC-OAR-FERMIONS). The hard-coded lit_paper_ids list in scripts/build-citation-index.py for the osborne-stottmeister atom is incorrect for this SRC-*. Need to: verify against papers.sqlite, fix the hardcoded mapping in build-citation-index.py, re-emit CITATION_INDEX in P3.4 (or earlier). Discovered via ground-truth check (CLAUDE.md Rule 7) of references/text/OsborneContinuumLimitsQuantumLatticeSystems.txt first-page metadata.

title: LB-1: MMA enumerate_fusion_trees incomplete for non-multiplicity-free categories
description: Bug in microscopic-mobile-anyons/src/MobileAnyons/hilbert.jl:42-68 — treats dim Hom > 0 as Boolean rather than enumerating multiplicity-vertex basis. Invisible for Fibonacci/Ising/sVec (multiplicity-free); blocking for any extension. Discovered in P1.4-early Hilbert-space deep-dive (stocktake/reports/opus-hilbert-bridge.md §3.5). Fix during Phase 8 (MMA migration) at P8.4: port with multiplicity counting, or at minimum add TODO marker + RESEARCH_NOTES entry. See RESEARCH_NOTES.md §Latent bugs LB-1.

title: Promote per-atom editorial decisions in build-citation-index.py to YAML
description: Reviewer of P2.8 (commit 1113ddc) MINOR-2 finding: per-atom lit_paper_ids / src_ids / lit_bibkeys are hard-coded in scripts/build-citation-index.py source. Defended by in-line comments and post-hoc resolve_discharges() warnings, but the initial choice is buried in Python. Promote to scripts/citation_atoms.yaml so editorial decisions are auditable independently of the script body. Defer until Phase 3 when \unchecked discharge work substantially changes the mappings.

title: CITATION_INDEX vs PRD undischarged-set asymmetry (Koo-Saleur 1994 grouping)
description: Reviewer of P2.8 (commit 1113ddc) MINOR-1 finding: PRD §Known limitations lists Koo-Saleur 1994 original as undischarged (no local PDF); CITATION_INDEX folds Koo-Saleur into a 'partial' atom because paper#590 (2017 follow-up) is in literature/. Both classifications are internally coherent but they don't trivially reconcile. Add explicit cross-reference in PRD §Known limitations and in CITATION_INDEX koo-saleur atom Notes so the two views align by construction. Trivial doc-only fix; bundle with next PRD refresh.
notes: Resolved via P3.3 RESEARCH_NOTES acquisition entry for koo-saleur (aq:koo-saleur). The entry explicitly states: paper#590 (arXiv:1706.01436, 2017 follow-up applying the Koo-Saleur formula numerically) is NOT a substitute for the 1994 Koo-Saleur original (Nucl. Phys. B 426, arXiv:hep-th/9312156, which derives and proves the lattice-Virasoro convergence result summary.tex conj:KS attributes). The 1994 original remains the acquisition target. The PRD §Known limitations classification ("Koo-Saleur 1994 original undischarged") and the CITATION_INDEX status ("partial" because paper#590 is in lit DB) are now reconciled-by-construction via the RESEARCH_NOTES anchor: paper#590 supports the partial status for numerical-application claims, but the 1994 paper is the acquisition target for the convergence-claim discharge. When the 1994 PDF lands and is discharged in P3.2-style, paper#590 becomes a supporting multi-cite (osborne-stottmeister pattern) and the asymmetry collapses.
close_reason: Closed

Update MIGRATION_PLAN.md:157 to match the as-implemented routing so future agents don't get confused. Trivial doc-only fix; should be bundled with any future MIGRATION_PLAN refresh.

The ground-truth file lives at /home/tobias/Projects/microscopic-mobile-anyons/archive/v0/docs/hilbert_space.md:39. Per CLAUDE.md file map this directory is reserved for the deprecated-MMA snapshot import scheduled in a later phase.

P1.9 mitigation: CONVENTIONS (a) Reasoning and Translation rules now clarify that the in-repo directory is a placeholder and name the ground-truth file path.

Follow-up action (this issue): once the deprecated-MMA snapshot is imported into archive/mma-archive-v0-snapshot/, verify that the cited path hilbert_space.md:39 actually contains the expected '0-indexed simple reference' content (X_0 = 1). If line numbers have drifted or the file has been renamed, update CONVENTIONS (a) to match. Blocks on the snapshot import step (likely Phase 2 or later).

title: LB-3: Audit GLOSSARY for fabricated TensorCategories function names
description: Per P1.8 reviewer finding (Edit 2 + follow-up #2, stocktake/reports/opus-glossary-p1.8-review.md): the P1.8 first-pass GLOSSARY draft fabricated the TensorCategories.jl function name 'multiplication_table' (caught by the reviewer and fixed in the same P1.8 commit). Class concern: are there other plausible-sounding but fabricated TensorCategories.jl API references elsewhere in GLOSSARY/CONVENTIONS or in future text additions? Action: run a bulk grep of all referenced TensorCategories.jl symbols against the actual TensorCategories.jl API (or against MMA's src/test usage) and flag any that don't appear. Best done as a Phase-8 pre-migration audit, or as a one-shot scripts/check-tensor-categories-api.py. Out of P1.8 scope.

title: LB-2: Add MMA test for dense Ising c=1/2 sector
description: Per P1.8 reviewer finding (Edit 5, stocktake/reports/opus-glossary-p1.8-review.md): MMA's def:dense GLOSSARY entry currently has no test coverage. The two related test files test/test_golden_chain.jl and test/test_golden_chain_cft.jl exercise the **Fibonacci** golden chain at c=7/10 (tricritical Ising fixed point), not the categorical **Ising** dense sigma-chain that should flow to c=1/2 critical Ising. Filing as a Phase-8-gated follow-up: add a new MMA test that runs enumerate_configs_hc(L, L, ising_category()) for L=4..12 and verifies the c=1/2 spectrum via Cardy finite-size fit. Out of P1.8 scope; blocks on Phase 8 (MMA migration).
